purpose: Define configurable email account linking, traceability, message logging, thread association and future provider integration without reading or sending real email.
configurationRules: Email account linking must be configurable per tenant and optionally per project/team.
Internal email traceability can start as manual log or future connector, depending on authorization.
TyA may use little email operationally, but internal teams may use email and need traceability.
Email traceability must associate threads/messages to operational entities when possible.
No real OAuth, IMAP, SMTP, Gmail or Outlook connection is authorized by this contract.
Sensitive data must be redacted or protected according to future privacy policy before storage.
emailOutboxRules: Email outbox is separate from notification outbox but may reference the same eventType and relatedEntity.
Drafts may be prepared but not sent unless explicit future authorization exists.
If email provider is not connected, status remains manual_log or blocked_by_gate.
All email actions must preserve actor, timestamp, related entity and auditTrail.
privacyAndSecurityRules: Do not store raw sensitive personal data unless policy explicitly allows it.
Do not store BCC recipients in shopper/client visible contexts.
Do not ingest email attachments into Storage without explicit policy and antivirus/privacy review.
Do not expose internal email threads to clients or shoppers unless specifically shared.
Do not connect personal or production mailboxes during documentation phase.

newLessons: How to link an email to a postulation, visit, client or support request
Difference between manual email log and future provider sync
What email data can and cannot be stored
How email relates to notification outbox

hardStops: Do not connect real email accounts from this contract.
Do not read real emails from this contract.
Do not send real emails from this contract.
Do not write Firestore from this contract.
Do not store full email bodies or attachments without privacy policy.
Do not expose internal email traces to external users by default.
